characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The patriarch of the Hendrickson Household, husband of Barbara, Nicki, and Margene. A 'lost boy' kicked out at the age of fourteen from the Juniper Creek compound, he initially rejected polygamy and married Barb monogamously but, before the series began, rediscovered a belief for polygamy, marrying Nicki, and then Margene. Runs a successful hardware store named Home Plus, which, at the start of the series, has two locations, with plans for another. Bumbling Dad: Frequently unaware of the conflicts and goings on within his home and family. Papa Wolf: For all his flaws as a parent, Bill is protective of his family. Upon being told of Alby stalking his daughter, Sarah, he immediately finds and beats him up.

characters.name: Bill
characters.description: The patriarch of the Hendrickson Household, husband of Barbara, Nicki, and Margene. A 'lost boy' kicked out at the age of fourteen from the Juniper Creek compound, he initially rejected polygamy and married Barb monogamously but, before the series began, rediscovered a belief for polygamy, marrying Nicki, and then Margene. Runs a successful hardware store named Home Plus, which, at the start of the series, has two locations, with plans for another. Bumbling Dad: Frequently unaware of the conflicts and goings on within his home and family. Papa Wolf: For all his flaws as a parent, Bill is protective of his family. Upon being told of Alby stalking his daughter, Sarah, he immediately finds and beats him up.

characters.name: Barb
characters.description: Bill's first wife, who he is legally married to. Raised in the LDS, Barb and Bill were initially monogamously married, but, following a bout with Uterine cancer which left her unable to conceive, Bill began to believe in the 'Principle' of polygamy, marrying Nicki Grant, and then Margene Heffman. Converting for Love: Originally raised in the mainstream Mormon church, which rejects the notion of polygamy, Barb eventually agrees to live the 'principle' of polygamy, before the beginning of the series, distancing herself from the mainstream Mormon church. Top Wife: As the first wife and legal wife, Barbara holds an amount of seniority and authority over the other wives.

characters.name: Nicki
characters.description: Bill's second wife, who married Bill after caring for Barb during her illness and convalescence. Raised in Juniper Creek, the daughter of the prophet Roman Grant. Female Misogynist: The most conservative of Bill's wives. Raised as a fundamentalist Mormon, who believes a woman's place is in the home. Money Dumb: Due, in part, to being raised in a fundamentalist Mormon Compound, Nicki doesn't have a very good grasp on how to handle money and credit cards. Retail Therapy: Early on in the series, Nicki discovers herself to have $58,000 dollars in debt across nine credit cards. Throughout the show, Nicki has difficulty spending money wisely, and, instead, frequently spends money on expensive gifts for herself and others. In a very genuine example of this trope, it's heavily implied that she does this to compensate for her loveless marriage. The Un-Favorite: In between Bill's first wife and his third wife, Nicki frequently considers herself the wife least loved by Bill, not without reason. Her brother, Alby, also claims her to be an unfavorite of her father.

characters.name: Margie
characters.description: Bill's third and youngest wife. Originally brought into the family to babysit for Nicki, only for Bill to marry her. Frequently more naive and immature than the other wives. Age-Gap Romance: The youngest of Bill's wives, whom he married when she was16, although Bill believed her to be 18 Precision F-Strike: In contrast tothe rest of the castMargene is the one who swears, albeit infrequently, and only when very stressed. This is justified by her not growing up as a Mormon or fundamentalist Mormon like most of the cast, for whom swearing is strongly discouraged.

characters.name: Sarah
characters.description: The eldest daughter of the Henrickson household. A non-believer in polygamy. Daddy's Girl: Of all of Bill's children, she is the one consistently shown to have the closest relationship with him. Only Sane Man: Frequently the most reasonable of the Henrickson household, despite being a teenager.

characters.name: Ben Henrickson
characters.description: Bill and Barb's oldest son, a hormonal teenage boy. Save You From Becoming Your Parent: In season 2, discovers a desire to practice polygamy, at first in order to marry Brynn, after heloses his virginity to her, and then for twin sisters from the compound, much to the disapproval of Barb.
